judgment.summary: High growth expectations are priced into Tesla's stock, with strong revenue and earnings growth but also high valuation multiples and modest margins.
judgment.earningsRecap: Last quarter beat estimates by 17.2%; next earnings date is 2026-07-22.
positives: Revenue growth 15.8% YoY and quarterly earnings growth 16.6% indicate strong top-line momentum.
Analyst growth estimates for the current fiscal year are robust at 23.4%.
Significant cash reserves of $44.74B provide a buffer.
Strong free cash flow of $5.25B TTM.
Consensus analyst rating is 'buy' with a mean target price suggesting potential upside.
negatives: Extremely high trailing P/E of 390.13 and forward P/E of 167.89 suggest the stock is richly valued.
Operating margin of 4.2% and profit margin of 3.9% are relatively thin.
Return on equity of 4.9% and return on assets of 2.2% are modest.
High Debt/Equity ratio of 18.74 indicates significant leverage.
Beta of 1.79 suggests higher volatility than the broader market.

news.paragraph: Recent headlines focus on SpaceX's board composition, which is not directly related to Tesla's operational performance or upcoming catalysts. Traders should look past this tangential news and focus on Tesla's own upcoming earnings report, which is the primary event risk for the stock in the near term.

headlines.title: Factbox-IPO-bound SpaceX's board is stacked with Musk empire loyalists

examples.body: $TSLA BTD 450+++, shorts are pilling up at the bottom
$TSLA For everybody who thought I was crazy when I said lastnight to buy puts and short this shit. You owe me a apology LOL
$TSLA 412.81 Massive volume block. The highest probability reload zone.
